title: pleopeltis rhomboidea
intro: Pleopeltis rhomboidea, also known as the rhombic polypody or rhombic polypody fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to the southeastern United States, commonly found in shaded, humid environments. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Pleopeltis rhomboidea include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making it a useful treatment for skin irritations and minor injuries. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's extract in topical creams and ointments for external use.
